config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama; kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu ditempatkan di atas hak pribadi, itu mudah sekali dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan dasar setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dihormati, lalu kepentingan umum diatur dengan adil dan proporsional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From a United States perspective, our history makes me wary of any argument that says the collective can override personal rights too easily, because that has often been used to justify discrimination, censorship, and abuse of power. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every situation, since public safety and basic order do matter, but the burden should be on society to prove a very strong need before limiting them. In my view, the default should be protecting the person first, not treating individuals as expendable for some vague greater good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kecil, membungkam kritik, atau membenarkan kebijakan yang tidak adil. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keselamatan bersama, tetapi pembatasan hak harus sangat ketat, transparan, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan jadi alasan umum untuk mengorbankan martabat orang. Justru masyarakat yang kuat itu terbentuk ketika hak individu dilindungi, karena tanpa itu kepentingan bersama mudah berubah menjadi alat kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From a United States perspective, I am especially suspicious of that idea because history shows how easily “the common good” can become a cover for censorship, discrimination, and abuse of power. Public safety and health can justify narrow limits sometimes, but those limits should be exceptional, clearly justified, and tightly controlled, not treated as the default. A society is strongest when it protects individual rights first and only restricts them when there is a truly compelling, specific need.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan hak orang kecil dan membungkam kritik. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi kalau ia dijadikan prioritas mutlak, batasnya mudah kabur dan akhirnya justru membuka jalan bagi penyalahgunaan kuasa. Menurut saya, yang benar adalah hak individu menjadi fondasi, lalu pembatasan hanya boleh dilakukan secara sangat terbatas, transparan, dan benar-benar perlu untuk keselamatan atau ketertiban yang nyata. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling sering mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi warga sambil tetap menjaga kepentingan bersama secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From a United States perspective, that language makes me uneasy because it has too often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach under the banner of the common good. I do accept that public safety, health, and basic order can sometimes justify limits on individual rights, but those limits should be narrow, specific, and heavily justified. In my view, a free society is measured by how well it protects the individual first, not by how easily it asks people to sacrifice their rights for a vague collective goal.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
